matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a BDNF neurotrophin-plasticity thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to activity-dependent BDNF secretion, synaptic plasticity, memory, mood/stress association studies, exercise-response biology, or TrkB pathway signaling. This is not a psychiatric or cognitive diagnosis.

variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed rs6265 (BDNF Val66Met) dosage suggests this sample carries the BDNF prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests a BDNF neurotrophin-plasticity thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to activity-dependent BDNF secretion, synaptic plasticity, memory, mood/stress association studies, exercise-response biology, or TrkB pathway signaling. This is not a psychiatric or cognitive diagnosis. This specific marker is curated as BDNF activity-dependent secretion marker. rs6265 (BDNF Val66Met) is curated as a BDNF marker for Synaptic plasticity and learning/memory research, Mood, stress, and neuropsychiatric association context. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the neurotrophin signaling, TrkB activation, synaptic plasticity, dendritic remodeling, and activity-dependent neuronal survival pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched rs6265 (BDNF Val66Met) at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the BDNF prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Can modify or tag neurotrophin signaling, TrkB activation, synaptic plasticity, dendritic remodeling, and activity-dependent neuronal survival.

methylation_sources.label: Whitelist mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses the curated whitelist probes stored in the interpretation database for the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: Gene-name-related mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses rows whose gene annotation explicitly names the current gene.

methylation_sources.label: All numeric-row mean beta
methylation_sources.description: Uses every numeric beta value that survived preprocessing for the current sample.

cases.label: Gene variant found
cases.prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a BDNF neurotrophin-plasticity thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to activity-dependent BDNF secretion, synaptic plasticity, memory, mood/stress association studies, exercise-response biology, or TrkB pathway signaling. This is not a psychiatric or cognitive diagnosis. An observed BDNF variant places this sample in the curated BDNF research context. The local BDNF bundle is neuroplasticity and neuropsychiatric-research oriented. Common BDNF variants are trait modifiers and should not be treated as deterministic psychiatric or cognitive predictions. Val66Met is studied for activity-dependent BDNF secretion, synaptic plasticity, memory, stress, mood, and exercise-response contexts.
cases.rationale: This is the base thesis that activates as soon as any promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample.
research_focus: Mood, stress, and neuropsychiatric association context

cases.label: Gene variant found + high whitelist mean beta
cases.prediction: When a BDNF variant is paired with high methylation in the whitelist mean beta, the sample best fits a combined regulatory-context thesis. This pattern leans toward stronger regulatory restraint or reduced local accessibility around the locus. The variant side of the thesis is: The variant observed in this sample suggests a BDNF neurotrophin-plasticity thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to activity-dependent BDNF secretion, synaptic plasticity, memory, mood/stress association studies, exercise-response biology, or TrkB pathway signaling. This is not a psychiatric or cognitive diagnosis. BDNF methylation should be treated as local regulatory context around a neurotrophin signaling, TrkB activation, synaptic plasticity, dendritic remodeling, and activity-dependent neuronal survival gene. Promoter-proximal methylation can support expression-potential review, but it should not replace genotype, RNA, protein, or phenotype data. The local BDNF bundle is neuroplasticity and neuropsychiatric-research oriented. Common BDNF variants are trait modifiers and should not be treated as deterministic psychiatric or cognitive predictions. Val66Met is studied for activity-dependent BDNF secretion, synaptic plasticity, memory, stress, mood, and exercise-response contexts.
